description: US B-2 Spirit stealth bombers struck the IRGC Aerospace Force headquarters compound in northern Tehran with GBU-57 Massive Ordnance Penetrators, destroying underground command bunkers. At least 40 senior IRGC officers were killed including two brigadier generals.

Simultaneous cruise missile salvo targeted the Supreme Leader's residential and administrative complex in the Niavaran district of northern Tehran. Iranian state media confirmed significant structural damage but claimed Khamenei was relocated hours prior.

JASSM-ER cruise missiles launched from B-1B Lancers struck the Iranian Ministry of Defense and Armed Forces Logistics compound, collapsing multiple buildings and destroying the central communications hub.

Social media reports and satellite imagery suggest a military logistics depot adjacent to Imam Khomeini International Airport was struck overnight. Secondary explosions were visible for several hours, suggesting ammunition stores were hit.

Israeli F-35I Adir jets penetrated Iranian airspace and delivered precision-guided munitions on the Isfahan Nuclear Technology Center and uranium conversion facility. The strike destroyed centrifuge assembly halls and contaminated surrounding areas with low-level radioactive material.

US Navy Tomahawk cruise missiles launched from destroyers in the Arabian Sea struck the Isfahan power generation station supplying electricity to remaining nuclear infrastructure. The strike caused widespread blackouts across Isfahan province.

Multiple waves of Israeli F-35I and F-15I aircraft delivered GBU-57 bunker busters on the deeply buried Natanz uranium enrichment facility, the crown jewel of Operation Rising Lion. IAEA later confirmed the underground centrifuge halls suffered catastrophic structural collapse.

The hardened Fordow Fuel Enrichment Plant, buried deep inside a mountain near Qom, was struck by Israeli penetrator munitions in a sustained 45-minute assault. While full destruction was not achieved, all access tunnels were collapsed and the facility rendered inoperable.

US Navy Tomahawk missiles struck the Bushehr Nuclear Power Plant complex, targeting the reactor's cooling systems and auxiliary buildings. Iran accused the US of risking a nuclear catastrophe. IAEA emergency teams were dispatched to assess radiological contamination.

Massive US air and naval strike on the IRIN (Islamic Republic of Iran Navy) main base at Bandar Abbas. B-2 bombers and carrier-based F/A-18s destroyed drydocks, missile boat pens, and the naval headquarters building. Three Iranian frigates were sunk at their moorings.

Israeli Air Force F-15I Ra'am aircraft struck Shahid Doran Airbase near Shiraz, targeting hardened aircraft shelters housing Iranian Su-35 fighters recently delivered by Russia. At least 6 aircraft were destroyed on the ground along with radar installations.
casualties: 18 air force personnel killed, 6 aircraft destroyed

description: US cruise missiles struck the IRGC Ground Forces regional headquarters in Tabriz, destroying the main command building and adjacent barracks. The strike was part of a broader campaign to degrade IRGC command and control across northwestern Iran.

Unconfirmed reports from Iranian social media indicate explosions near a suspected IRGC Quds Force training and logistics facility on the outskirts of Mashhad. The Pentagon declined to comment. Satellite imagery showed burn scars consistent with cruise missile impacts.

US strikes targeted the IRGC 92nd Armored Division base near Ahvaz in Khuzestan province, destroying vehicle depots, fuel storage, and a mobile missile launcher staging area. Secondary explosions continued for hours after the initial strike.

A US JASSM-ER missile struck a building in Minab that the Pentagon identified as a suspected IRGC missile logistics warehouse. The building was in fact a girls' secondary school in session. Between 85 and 180 schoolgirls aged 12-17 were killed in the deadliest single incident of the war, sparking global outrage and mass protests.

Israeli strikes hit the Parchin military research complex southeast of Tehran, long suspected of housing nuclear weapons research facilities. Massive explosions were reported as underground chambers containing explosive testing equipment were destroyed.

Residents in the industrial city of Karaj reported multiple explosions overnight near a centrifuge component manufacturing facility previously linked to Iran's nuclear program. Israel did not confirm the strike but satellite imagery showed significant damage to factory buildings.

Multiple explosions reported in the outskirts of the holy city of Qom near suspected IRGC underground missile storage tunnels. Iran condemned the strikes near religious sites. The US neither confirmed nor denied the reports.

The IR-40 heavy water reactor at Arak, capable of producing weapons-grade plutonium, was struck by Israeli F-35I jets during the opening hours of Operation Rising Lion. The reactor vessel was breached and cooling systems destroyed, rendering the facility permanently inoperable.

US B-2 bombers struck a hardened underground IRGC ballistic missile base near Dezful in Khuzestan province, targeting a facility believed to house Shahab-3 and Emad medium-range ballistic missiles. Massive secondary explosions indicated missile propellant detonation.

Iran launched Operation True Promise 3 in retaliation for Operation Rising Lion, firing over 300 ballistic missiles and cruise missiles at Israel. Several Emad and Shahab-3 missiles penetrated Iron Dome and Arrow-3 defenses, impacting near the Kirya military headquarters compound in central Tel Aviv. Significant structural damage to surrounding buildings was reported.

Iranian ballistic missile debris and at least one warhead reportedly impacted near Ben Gurion International Airport, forcing a complete shutdown of Israel's main aviation hub. Damage to runway infrastructure was reported but not officially confirmed by Israeli authorities.

Hezbollah launched a sustained barrage of Fajr-5 and Zelzal heavy rockets at Haifa, striking the port area and nearby oil refineries. A direct hit on a fuel storage tank triggered a massive fire that burned for three days. Haifa port operations were halted indefinitely.

In the largest single missile attack of the war, Iran launched 9 successive waves of ballistic missiles at Jerusalem over a 4-hour period, overwhelming Iron Dome and David's Sling batteries. Multiple impacts were recorded across the city, including near the Old City walls. International condemnation was swift from all parties.

Iranian Shahab-3 missiles targeted Nevatim Airbase south of Beer Sheva, home to Israel's F-35I Adir fleet, during Operation True Promise 3. While most missiles were intercepted by Arrow-3, two warheads impacted the base perimeter, damaging taxiways and a maintenance hangar.

Unconfirmed reports indicate Iranian Khorramshahr missiles were directed at the Negev Nuclear Research Center near Dimona. Israeli censors imposed a full blackout on reporting. Seismic sensors detected impacts in the area but Israel stated all inbound threats were neutralized.

A combined drone and ballistic missile swarm targeted Ramat David Airbase in the Jezreel Valley during Operation True Promise 3. Over 50 Shahed-136 drones saturated air defenses while Fateh-110 missiles struck the runway and flight line, destroying 3 F-16I aircraft in their shelters.

An Iranian Emad ballistic missile warhead struck a residential apartment block in the city of Beit Shemesh after evading Iron Dome interceptors. The four-story building collapsed entirely, killing nine civilians including three children. Rescue operations continued for 36 hours.
source: IDF Home Front Command / Israeli Media

description: Residents of Bat Yam, south of Tel Aviv, reported loud explosions and missile debris falling in a commercial district. Israeli military censors restricted reporting but social media videos showed shattered storefronts and car fires consistent with interceptor debris impacts.

During Operation True Promise 3, missile impacts were reported near the Weizmann Institute of Science in Rehovot. Social media footage showed a large crater in an open field approximately 500 meters from the campus. No structural damage to the institute was confirmed.

Iranian cruise missiles struck Ashdod Port, Israel's largest cargo port, hitting container yards and a grain silo. The strike was part of Iran's strategy to disrupt Israeli supply lines. Port operations were suspended and shipping routes diverted to Eilat.

Hezbollah launched a massive barrage of over 200 Fajr-5 and Katyusha rockets at the northern Israeli city of Kiryat Shmona in coordination with Iran's missile offensive. The city, already largely evacuated, suffered widespread destruction to residential buildings, schools, and infrastructure.

Iran struck Al-Udeid Air Base, the largest US military facility in the Middle East, with a salvo of Shahab-3 ballistic missiles and Paveh cruise missiles. THAAD and Patriot batteries intercepted most inbound missiles but at least 3 struck the flight line, damaging parked KC-135 tanker aircraft and a C-17 transport.

Iranian Fateh-110 short-range ballistic missiles struck Camp Arifjan, the primary US Army logistics hub in Kuwait. Strikes hit vehicle staging areas and a warehouse complex, destroying dozens of MRAPs and supply containers. Patriot interceptors neutralized 8 of 12 inbound missiles.

An Iranian Emad ballistic missile struck a US Navy logistics support area at Port Shuaiba in Kuwait, hitting a personnel quarters building during the early morning hours. Six US service members were killed in their sleep, making it the single deadliest US loss in the opening hours of Iran's retaliatory strikes.

Iranian cruise missiles and Shahed-136 drones targeted Al Dhafra Air Base near Abu Dhabi, home to US F-35A fighters and MQ-9 Reaper drones. UAE and US air defenses intercepted most threats but 2 cruise missiles struck taxiway infrastructure, temporarily halting flight operations.
source: CENTCOM Statement / UAE Ministry of Defense

description: Iranian Fateh-313 precision-guided missiles struck the vicinity of Erbil Air Base in Iraqi Kurdistan, targeting US special operations forces and intelligence facilities. Several missiles impacted the base perimeter, destroying communications equipment and a drone hangar.

In a repeat of the January 2020 strikes, Iran launched 15 Qiam-1 ballistic missiles at Al-Asad Air Base in Anbar province. US forces had partially evacuated to hardened shelters after the initial March 1 attacks, but 4 missiles struck occupied facilities, causing significant casualties.

Unconfirmed reports indicate Iranian missiles or drones targeted Naval Support Activity Bahrain in the Juffair district of Manama, headquarters of the US Fifth Fleet. Bahraini and US officials declined to comment, but local residents reported air defense activations and distant explosions overnight.

Iranian cruise missiles struck the Ras Laffan Industrial City, the world's largest LNG export terminal, causing fires at two liquefaction trains and damaging a loading berth. Global LNG prices surged 40% within hours. Qatar condemned the attack as an act of economic warfare against a neutral state.
source: Qatari Government Statement / Energy Industry Sources

description: Unverified reports circulated on social media of explosions near the Jebel Ali port complex in Dubai, the largest port in the Middle East. UAE authorities issued a terse denial, but commercial shipping satellite data showed vessels diverting away from the port for 18 hours.

Iranian Mohajer-6 armed drones struck a suspected Israeli intelligence listening post near the Azerbaijani exclave of Nakhchivan, bordering Iran and Turkey. Azerbaijan condemned the violation of its sovereignty and recalled its ambassador from Tehran. NATO member Turkey placed forces on heightened alert.
source: Azerbaijani Ministry of Defense / Turkish Media

description: Israeli Air Force conducted intensive strikes on Hezbollah command infrastructure in Beirut's Dahieh (southern suburbs), targeting underground bunkers used for coordinating rocket attacks on northern Israel. Entire city blocks were flattened in what Lebanon called a disproportionate escalation.
casualties: 65+ killed including Hezbollah commanders, 200+ civilian casualties

description: Israeli strikes hit suspected Hezbollah rocket launcher positions and weapons storage sites in and around the ancient coastal city of Tyre in southern Lebanon. A UN peacekeeping outpost was damaged in the strikes, prompting UNIFIL to evacuate remaining personnel from the area.

US Tomahawk cruise missiles and MQ-9 Reaper drone strikes targeted Houthi (Ansar Allah) senior military leadership compounds in the Yemeni capital Sana'a. The strikes killed a senior Houthi military commander responsible for coordinating anti-shipping missile attacks in the Red Sea and Bab el-Mandeb strait.
casualties: 20+ Houthi fighters killed including senior commander

description: Iranian missile struck a building in the southern Syrian city of Sweida, killing four people. The strike highlighted the expanding geographic scope of Iranian retaliatory attacks beyond traditional conflict zones.

An Iranian-made drone damaged the runway at the British RAF base at Akrotiri, Cyprus. This marked the first attack on European NATO territory in the conflict, raising fears of further escalation beyond the Middle East.

A military projectile struck a residential facility in the Al-Kharj area south of Riyadh, causing the first civilian deaths in Saudi Arabia during the conflict. The attack marked a significant escalation of Iranian strikes against Gulf states.
A hostile drone struck a desalination plant in Salman Industrial City, Bahrain, causing a fire that killed one worker. The attack on critical water infrastructure drew international condemnation.

The IDF launched wide-scale strikes targeting Tehran's energy resources and regime targets, marking a new phase in the conflict. Multiple energy infrastructure sites across the capital were hit in coordinated waves of precision strikes.

Qatar intercepted 17 ballistic missiles and 6 drones launched by Iran targeting the Doha area. All incoming projectiles were neutralized by Qatari and allied air defense systems, preventing any casualties or damage on the ground.

An Iranian ballistic missile struck a residential area in the Sitra district of Bahrain, causing significant damage to apartment buildings and injuring 32 civilians. One person was killed in the attack. The strike marked the deadliest Iranian attack on Bahraini civilian areas since the conflict began, drawing strong condemnation from the GCC.

A NATO Patriot battery stationed in southern Turkey intercepted an Iranian ballistic missile that entered Turkish airspace. The missile, believed to be a stray or deliberately provocative launch, was destroyed before reaching Turkish territory. NATO invoked Article 4 consultations, with Turkey condemning the airspace violation as a hostile act.
source: NATO Press Release / Turkish Ministry of Defense

description: US-Israeli airstrike on residential buildings in eastern Tehran killed at least 40 people. Earlier attacks on nearby oil facilities blanketed the capital in toxic smoke. One of the deadliest single attacks on civilian areas since the conflict began.
Five people killed and several injured in a US-Israeli air strike on a residential building in the city of Arak in western Iran, according to Iranian state news agency ISNA.

Strikes on Isfahan damaged multiple UNESCO World Heritage sites including Naqsh-e Jahan Square, Chehel Sotoun palace, Ali Qapu, Shah Mosque, and Teymouri Hall. International cultural organizations condemned the destruction of irreplaceable heritage.
Iranian army launched drone attack targeting an Israeli oil and gas refinery and fuel tanks in Haifa. Israeli air defenses engaged multiple incoming drones. Damage assessment ongoing.
Saudi Arabia's Defense Ministry confirmed its military intercepted and destroyed an Iranian drone east of al-Kharj governorate, south of Riyadh. No casualties or damage reported.
A building in Seef district of Manama damaged by reported Iranian drone strike. The attack on the commercial district deepened fears of Iranian targeting of civilian infrastructure in Gulf states.

Iran deploys Khoramshahr super-heavy ballistic missiles for the first time in its 37th wave of attacks. The Khoramshahr is Iran's most powerful conventional missile with a 1,800km range and 1,800kg warhead. Multiple intercepted by Arrow-3 system.

3 more commercial ships attacked by IRGC Navy forces in the Strait of Hormuz, bringing total to 14 vessels damaged or sunk since the war began. Shipping traffic down 95% from pre-war levels. 330+ vessels now at anchor.

Iranian explosive-laden boats attacked two fuel tankers (Marshall Islands-flagged Safesea Vishnu and Malta-flagged Zefyros) in Iraqi waters, setting them ablaze. 1 crew member killed, 25 evacuated. Iraq shut down oil port operations and condemned it as 'sabotage' and 'flagrant violation of sovereignty.'

Thai-flagged Mayuree Naree dry bulk vessel struck by two projectiles while transiting the Strait of Hormuz, causing fire and engine room damage. 3 crew missing (believed trapped), 20 evacuated to Oman. Japan-flagged ONE Majesty sustained minor damage 25nm NW of Ras Al Khaimah. Two more vessels also struck. Total ships attacked since war began: at least 16.

Israeli airstrike hit apartment in Aisha Bakkar area of central Beirut, a densely populated neighborhood near one of the city's biggest shopping malls. Fireball erupted on 7th-8th floors. Separately, strike on Ramlet al-Baida seafront killed at least 7 people.

Hezbollah rocket struck Moshav Haniel in central Israel, virtually destroying a home and creating a large crater. Residents survived by reaching reinforced safe room during 90-second siren. Building destroyed except for safe room.
Hezbollah rockets struck Nahariya as part of the largest coordinated barrage of the war (200+ rockets, 20 drones). Part of Iran-Hezbollah 'joint integrated operation.'
Hezbollah claimed striking Beit Lid military base with rockets and drones as part of coordinated Iran-Hezbollah operation.
Hezbollah claimed attack on Glilot base, headquarters of Israel's elite Unit 8200 intelligence unit near Tel Aviv. Part of joint operation with Iran.
Iran fired advanced ballistic missiles toward Tel Aviv as part of its 'most intense operation since the beginning of the war.' Sirens triggered across Tel Aviv metro area.
Iranian ballistic missiles targeted Haifa as part of the coordinated Iran-Hezbollah operation. Atlit naval commando base south of Haifa also targeted by Hezbollah drones.
Deadly attacks on two foreign oil tankers in Iraqi territorial waters, causing Iraq to shut down oil port operations. Further disrupts global energy supply.
Israeli overnight strikes hit Beirut's Corniche seafront — a designated evacuation area. At least 8 killed, dozens injured.
Israeli airstrikes on al-Nabi Shayth in Beqaa Valley. 6 injured, 2 Hezbollah members killed. Part of expanded operations past Litani River.
New wave of Israeli strikes hit Tehran. IDF announced 'wide-scale wave of strikes' targeting locations across Iran. Part of 6,000+ total targets struck since Feb 28.

Iran launched its most intense ballistic missile barrage of the war targeting Tel Aviv and Haifa, firing Ghadr, Emad, Kheybar Shekan, and Fattah hypersonic missiles. Israeli multi-layered defense systems intercepted all incoming projectiles. IDF confirmed no impacts on Israeli soil.
Iranian ballistic missiles targeted US military installations in Kuwait. Part of coordinated salvo also targeting Bahrain. Damage assessment pending.
Iranian ballistic missiles targeted US Naval Support Activity Bahrain and surrounding military infrastructure. Part of coordinated strikes on Gulf-based US installations.
Drone strike on coalition base near Makhmour, Erbil province, Iraq. One French soldier killed and several others wounded in the attack attributed to Iran-backed Iraqi militias.
Oil tankers struck in Iraqi waters near Basra. Part of Iran's expanding campaign targeting energy infrastructure and shipping in the Persian Gulf region.
Iranian ballistic missile struck the northern town of Zarzir. Woman moderately wounded by shrapnel, over 50 injured by broken glass. IDF opened probe into the incident.
Third Iranian ballistic missile intercepted by NATO air defenses over Turkish airspace near Adana. Residents woken by sirens at 3:25am. No casualties reported.
11 Iranian cluster missiles penetrated Israeli air defenses. One dropped approximately 70 submunitions over central Israel. 2 Israelis killed, multiple wounded.
Israel destroyed the Zrarieh Bridge over the Litani River — first acknowledged strike on civilian infrastructure in Lebanon. Part of expanded strike campaign hitting areas never previously targeted.
casualties: Part of campaign killing 25+ across Lebanon

description: Israeli targeted killing attempt in Beirut kills several. Part of expanded strike campaign across Beirut, southern and eastern Lebanon. 25+ killed total across Lebanon on Day 14.
Extensive Israeli/US airstrikes in Tehran — 'highest volume of strikes' per Hegseth. Explosions reported near Al-Quds Day rally. One woman killed. Part of 15,000+ total targets struck campaign.
casualties: 1 killed near rally, additional casualties expected

description: CENTCOM massive bombing raid on Kharg Island — 15+ explosions destroy army defenses, Joshen Sea Base, airport control tower, helicopter hangar. Oil infrastructure deliberately spared.
Missile strikes US Embassy compound helipad in Baghdad. Suicide drone targeted radar system first. IRGC claims 48th phase of retaliatory campaign.
NATO defenses intercept 3rd Iranian missile in Turkish airspace near Adana and Incirlik airbase. Sirens at 3:25 AM. Third such incident since the war began.
3 Iranian ballistic missiles fired at central Israel. No casualties. One struck open area. Iran's missile capability down 90%+ but continuing attacks.
Drone attack on Fujairah port causes fire, forces suspension of oil loading operations. Key alternative to Hormuz now disrupted.
IDF destroys Iran's primary space research center and air defense factories in Tehran. Part of 7,600+ Israeli strikes in Iran since Feb 28.
KC-135 Stratotanker crashes — all 6 crew killed. CENTCOM says not hostile fire. Iranian proxy claims responsibility.

casualties.note: All crew from Ohio ANG 121st Air Refueling Wing

description: 3 F-15E Strike Eagles downed in friendly fire incident over Kuwait. All 6 crew ejected safely.

casualties.note: All 6 crew ejected and recovered

description: CENTCOM strikes 90 military targets on Kharg Island — naval mine storage, missile bunkers, air defenses, naval base, airport control tower, helicopter hangar. Oil infrastructure deliberately spared.
Iranian retaliatory missile strike on Eilat — child moderately injured, 2 lightly hurt. Part of broader retaliatory salvo targeting central Israel, Jerusalem, and Gulf states.

casualties.note: 1 child moderately injured, 2 lightly hurt

description: Iranian drones hit Kuwait's Ahmad Al-Jaber Air Base as part of retaliatory strikes against Gulf state hosts of US forces.
Heavy overnight airstrikes on Isfahan area — part of coordinated multi-city bombardment
20 explosions reported near Shiraz during overnight strikes
Dezful Air Base targeted in overnight strikes
Heavy explosions reported in Hamedan during multi-city overnight bombing
IRGC launches 10 hypersonic missiles and drones at Al Dhafra Air Base targeting US forces
Israeli strike on residential building in northern Sidon, killing 1
IDF eliminates Hezbollah intel chief Hussein Makled in overnight Beirut strike
Iranian rocket strikes residential area in Holon, Tel Aviv District. Casualties reported.
US-Israeli strike on Isfahan industrial factory complex, killing at least 15 workers
US cruise missile strike damages Jask port facilities, targeting Iranian naval infrastructure
IDF wide-scale strikes destroy IRGC/Basij headquarters and command centers in Hamedan province, western Iran. Part of 200+ target wave.
Cluster munition strikes residential building in Bnei Brak. Upper floor of 4-story building hit. 3 wounded.
Baghdad International Airport hit by 5 rockets. Runway damaged, flights suspended. 5 wounded. US Embassy issues security alert.
Iranian drone/rocket attack on Ali Al Salem airbase destroys Italian Air Force MQ-9 Reaper in hangar. First NATO ally hardware loss. All personnel safe.
Iranian missiles targeting Dubai Marina area successfully intercepted by UAE air defense systems. No damage or casualties reported.
IDF launches ground operation in southern Lebanon with supporting airstrikes on Khiam. Hezbollah positions and infrastructure targeted as troops advance into the town.
Iranian drone strikes fuel storage tank near Dubai International Airport, causing massive fire. All flights suspended for 7+ hours. One of the world's busiest airports shut down.
IDF destroys Khamenei's personal aircraft at Mehrabad Airport military section. Satellite imagery shows destroyed hangars. Symbolic strike eliminating Supreme Leader's escape route.
Iranian drone/missile attack on Fujairah oil hub causes fire at petroleum storage facility. Continued targeting of UAE energy infrastructure.
Iranian ballistic missile strikes residential area in Al Bahia, Abu Dhabi. One civilian killed. First confirmed civilian fatality from Iranian strikes on UAE soil.

casualties.note: 1 civilian killed

description: 6 Iranian ballistic missiles strike military compound near Al-Kharj, south of Riyadh. 2 killed, 12 injured. Part of overnight barrage that included 60+ drones intercepted by Saudi air defenses.
Iranian missile fragments fell near Church of the Holy Sepulchre, National Library near Knesset, and East Jerusalem after interception. 1 minor injury.
Missile struck civilian vehicle in Al Bahyah area, killing one Palestinian national.
5 rockets hit Baghdad International Airport and surrounding area. Kataib Hezbollah claimed responsibility targeting Camp Victory. 5 wounded.
Air raid hit PMF headquarters in Jurf al-Sakhar, Iraq. 3 people injured.
Fajr-type missiles with cluster munition warheads. 10 locations damaged in Rishon Lezion, Lod, and Shoham. 1 woman injured when home destroyed.
Hezbollah rocket struck residential building causing fire. 6 wounded (2 adults, 4 children). Separate impact near Kibbutz Kabri wounded 1.
Iranian drone struck Fujairah Oil Industry Zone — second strike in 2 days. ADNOC suspended loading at UAE's only non-Hormuz oil export route.
IDF destroyed Supreme Leader's aircraft at Mehrabad International Airport. Plane used by senior Iranian officials for international flights.
Israeli jets struck Iran's Shah gas field at South Pars complex — world's largest natural gas field shared with Qatar. Major fire reported.
US drone strike on Baghdad's Green Zone killed IRGC Quds Force commander Brig. Gen. Hossein al-Askari. Al-Askari was coordinating militia operations across Iraq.
Targeted Israeli airstrike killed Ali Larijani, former Speaker of Iran's Parliament and senior adviser to Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei. Larijani was reportedly en route to a meeting.
Israeli strikes eliminated Basij paramilitary leadership in Tehran. Multiple senior commanders killed in coordinated assault on regime security apparatus.
Iranian cluster munitions struck 10+ locations in central Israel including Rishon LeZion. First confirmed use of cluster munitions in the conflict. Multiple casualties reported.
US airstrikes on Arak hit residential areas, killing at least 14 civilians including children. Iran reports widespread destruction in residential neighborhoods.
Israeli airstrikes on Beirut's Dahiyeh district targeting Hezbollah infrastructure as IDF ground forces advance in southern Lebanon.
Third consecutive strike on Fujairah oil loading zone. Oil operations suspended again. One commercial tanker reportedly hit.
IDF 91st Division ground forces entered southern Lebanon, taking Khiam and surrounding areas. First major Israeli ground operation in Lebanon since 2006.
Missile and interceptor fragments hit Temple Mount/Al-Aqsa Mosque, Church of Holy Sepulchre, Armenian Patriarchate, and Jewish Quarter. Warhead landed less than 1km from Temple Mount.
First combat use of Haj Qasem ballistic missile (1,400km range, MaRV-equipped). Part of 59th missile attack wave targeting Tel Aviv, Jerusalem, and Beit Shemesh.
Iran struck UAE's Shah gas field, setting it ablaze. The field supplies 20% of UAE natural gas. Major energy infrastructure attack.
Fujairah oil terminal hit for the 3rd time in 4 days. Kuwait-flagged tanker Gas Al Ahmadiah also struck near the port.
Pakistani national killed by falling debris from intercepted Iranian ballistic missile in Abu Dhabi's Bani Yas area.
Hezbollah rockets hit Nahariya causing fire. 6 people wounded. Part of 37+ attack waves from southern Lebanon.
Hezbollah rocket hit a residential home in Karmiel. Residents survived in safe room. 5 drones also launched at northern Israel.
IRGC Khorramshahr-4 and Qadr missiles hit Ramat Gan. Elderly couple killed in stairwell. Shrapnel hit Tel Aviv Savidor Center train station.
Projectile hit premises of Bushehr Nuclear Power Plant near operating unit. IAEA confirmed no reactor damage.
Coordinated drone and rocket attack on US Embassy. 1 of 3 drones struck outer wall.
Israeli strikes on Kafaat and Haret Hreik, Beirut. 6 killed, 24 injured.
Israeli strike on Haboush, Nabatieh. 3 killed, 8 missing.
Israeli strike on Baalbek. 4 killed, 7 injured.
US-Israeli strike on Arak killed 3-day-old infant, 2-year-old sister, mother, and grandmother.
IDF airstrike killed Intelligence Minister Esmail Khatib — third senior official in 48h.
Israeli jets struck gas storage tanks at South Pars — world's largest gas field. Two refineries halted.
Bachoura building destroyed after evacuation order. 6 killed.
Strikes on Basta and Zuqaq al-Blat — no warning issued. 6 killed, 24 injured.
Strike on densely populated area killed 7, injured 56.
Strikes on Tyre and Burj Shemali after mass evacuation order. 11,000 displaced.
CENTCOM used 5,000-lb deep penetrator munitions on hardened antiship missile sites.
Strike on judiciary facility in Larestan.
Iranian missiles struck Ras Laffan Industrial City — world's largest LNG facility. 'Extensive damage.' 1 hit after 4 intercepted.
Iranian missile hit beauty salon near Hebron. 3 killed including 2 women.
Israeli strikes on Baalbek killed 4, wounded 7.
Israeli strikes on Sahmar/Sohmor in Bekaa Valley killed 6.
Second strike on Ras Laffan in 12 hours. Sizeable fires.
Cluster missile killed foreign worker, injured 4+.
IAF strikes 4 gas treatment facilities at South Pars gas field, coordinated with US. Plants taken offline.
IDF first-ever Caspian Sea strike destroys 4 missile boats, corvette, and naval infrastructure at Bandar Anzali.
Overnight airstrike kills Intelligence Minister Esmail Khatib — 3rd senior official assassinated in 48 hours.
Iran revenge missile strike kills couple in their 70s in Ramat Gan apartment. Several injured across central Israel.
Iran attacks Ras Laffan LNG complex causing extensive damage. Shell Pearl GTL plant also hit. No casualties reported.
Iranian drone strikes Saudi SAMREF refinery in Red Sea port city of Yanbu. Saudi intercepted 6 drones before one got through.
Iranian drone targeting radar hits residential building in Al-Kharj, killing 2 foreign workers and injuring 12.
Abu Dhabi shuts Habshan gas facilities after hit by falling debris from intercepted Iranian strike. Bab field also affected.
Israeli strikes in Dorud city kill 7 and injure 56. Residential areas impacted.
Iranian ballistic missile shrapnel struck Bazan Group oil refinery after intercept. Fires extinguished, 45-minute power outage. No injuries.
Hezbollah rockets struck building in Kiryat Shmona. 4 wounded: 60-year-old man serious, 68-year-old woman moderate, 2 young men light.
Iranian missile fragments/cluster munitions struck a hair salon, killing 3 Palestinian women. A 4th woman also reported killed.
Iranian drone attacks on two Kuwaiti refinery units — Mina Al-Ahmadi and Mina Abdullah. Combined 730,000 bpd capacity. Fires extinguished, no casualties.
First-ever Israeli strike on Iran's Caspian Sea coast. 5 Iranian Navy missile boats, a headquarters, and a shipyard destroyed at Bandar Anzali port.

Second wave of Iranian ballistic missiles on Ras Laffan Industrial City. QatarEnergy CEO says 17% LNG export capacity knocked out for 3-5 years. Two LNG trains and one GTL facility damaged. $26B in damaged infrastructure.
Strikes on PMF 6th and 31st Brigade positions at Beiji HQ and Al Siniya checkpoint. 2 killed, several injured.
Overnight strikes on IRGC command centers — killed spokesman Gen. Naeini. 4th senior official killed this week.
Nowruz wave of strikes targeting 'Iranian terror regime infrastructure' — multiple explosions across Tehran.
Two waves of Iranian drones hit Kuwait's largest refinery (730K bpd) for 2nd consecutive day. Fires erupted, units shut down.
Drone struck SAMREF refinery (400K bpd, Saudi Aramco/ExxonMobil JV). Ballistic missile also intercepted targeting Yanbu Port.
UAE air defenses intercepted incoming Iranian fire over Dubai during Eid al-Fitr. Heavy explosions from successful interceptions.
Shrapnel from intercepted Iranian projectile caused warehouse fire. No injuries. Bahrain total: 139 missiles + 238 drones intercepted since war start.
Saudi air defenses intercepted Iranian missile over northern Al Jouf region.

IDF Nowruz strikes on Tehran — 'wave of strikes targeting infrastructure of the Iranian terror regime in the heart of Tehran.' IRGC spokesman Naini killed. Multiple explosions across eastern Tehran on Nowruz morning.

casualties.note: Naini confirmed killed; other casualties unknown

description: Iranian drones hit Kuwait's Mina Al-Ahmadi oil refinery complex (730,000 bpd) for the second time, sparking fires. Emergency crews worked to contain the blaze. Kuwait summoned Iran's ambassador.

casualties.note: No casualties confirmed; firefighting crews deployed

description: Iran renewed missile attacks on Israel after a brief lull on Nowruz Day 21. Sirens sounded from Haifa to the Galilee to the Lebanese border. Most intercepted by air defenses. Iran claimed missile production unaffected by war.

casualties.note: Air defense intercepts reported; no confirmed casualties from this wave

description: Iranian ballistic missile intercepted over Jerusalem; fragments from interception landed approximately 400 meters from the Western Wall in the Jewish Quarter of the Old City. No injuries reported. One of eight salvos launched by Iran since midnight on Nowruz/Eid al-Fitr.
Iranian ballistic missile impacted in Rehovot during Day 21 Nowruz missile barrage. Part of Iran's 8th consecutive salvo since midnight. No casualties reported.

Israeli airstrike in Beirut killed Hussein Makled, described as head of Hezbollah's intelligence headquarters. Israel simultaneously issued new evacuation orders for southern Lebanese villages ahead of anticipated expanded ground operations.

First Israeli strike on Iran's Caspian coast — IDF targeted Bandar Anzali naval base, destroying 5 Iranian Navy missile boats, striking a headquarters building, and damaging a shipyard. This opened a new geographic front in the war, demonstrating Israel's ability to strike across Iran's full extent. The Caspian Sea had previously been considered beyond reach.

casualties.note: Iranian Navy casualties not reported

description: Iran fired two intermediate-range ballistic missiles at Diego Garcia US-UK base — the longest-range Iranian strike ever attempted at ~4,000 km, double Iran's previously claimed capability. One failed mid-flight; US destroyer fired SM-3 interceptor at the second. Neither missile hit the base. Attack came hours after UK authorized US to use the base for Iran strikes.

casualties.note: Base not struck; both missiles intercepted/failed

description: Fragment from an intercepted Iranian ballistic missile struck a road and wall near Dung Gate in Jerusalem's Jewish Quarter — approximately 400 meters from the Western Wall and Al-Aqsa Mosque. No injuries from this specific impact. Part of Iran's 8th ballistic missile barrage of Day 21 (Nowruz), which targeted multiple Israeli cities.

casualties.note: No injuries from this fragment; 2 lightly wounded in separate Rehovot cluster bomb impact

description: Second consecutive day of Iranian drone strikes on Kuwait's Mina Al-Ahmadi oil refinery (730,000 bpd capacity). Two waves of drones sparked fires across multiple processing units. Mina Abdullah refinery also reportedly struck. Kuwait's air defenses were actively intercepting threats. The refinery is one of the largest in the Middle East.

casualties.note: No casualties reported; fire damage to processing units

description: Iranian ballistic missile with cluster warhead struck an empty daycare in Rishon Lezion on Day 22. No casualties. Missile was part of Iran's ongoing barrage against central Israel.

US-Israeli airstrike on Iran's Natanz uranium enrichment facility on Day 22. Iran's primary centrifuge complex. Iranian state media Tasnim reported 'no radioactive leakage.' Strike follows earlier attacks on Fordow and Isfahan nuclear sites.
weaponType: Precision guided munitions (likely GBU-57 MOP or JASSM-ER)

description: Second major US airstrike on Natanz uranium enrichment facility (Shahid Ahmadi Roshan complex). IAEA confirmed no off-site radiation increase. IDF denied involvement, suggesting unilateral US strike using bunker-buster munitions. Iran's Atomic Energy Organization confirmed the attack and said nuclear material was safe.

IDF struck a large-scale ballistic missile storage array in western Iran with 5 near-simultaneous strikes, killing a 'large number' of IRGC ballistic missile personnel. Overnight attacks also hit a central IRGC ballistic missile component production compound, a missile production storage facility, and a Ministry of Defense compound producing missile fuel. IDF stated these strikes 'significantly degrade' Iran's ballistic missile production capability.

casualties.note: Large number of IRGC missile personnel killed per IDF

description: Second consecutive day of Iranian drone strikes on Kuwait's Mina al-Ahmadi oil refinery (730,000 bpd capacity). Fires erupted across multiple units. Kuwait's national oil company shut down multiple units. The repeated targeting of the Gulf's largest refinery complex significantly impacted regional oil processing capacity.
Iranian IRBM direct hit center of Arad between apartment buildings — air defense failed to intercept. 74 injured (7 serious).
Iranian ballistic missile strikes Dimona — adjacent to Negev Nuclear Research Center. One-story building destroyed. IAEA confirmed no nuclear damage.

Iranian Khorramshahr-4 ballistic missile scored a direct hit on Arad, southern Israel. Three buildings sustained massive damage. IDF Arrow and David's Sling air defense systems failed to intercept — IDF opened formal probe. 74 total wounded: 7 serious (including 5-year-old girl), 15 moderate, 52 light. Fire ignited on 4th floor of residential building. Helicopters deployed for evacuation. IDF said missile was 'not an unfamiliar type' yet was not intercepted.

casualties.note: 74 wounded: 7 serious (including child), 15 moderate, 52 light

description: Iranian ballistic missile struck residential area of Dimona — the city adjacent to Israel's Negev Nuclear Research Center (~10 km away). IDF air defense failed to intercept. 47 hospitalized including a 12-year-old boy in serious condition. Three-story building collapsed; 12 distinct impact sites identified. IAEA confirmed no damage to nuclear facility and no abnormal radiation. First Iranian strike on Israel's nuclear city — IDF opening formal investigation into interception failure.

casualties.note: 47 hospitalized; child in serious condition; 3-story building collapsed

description: Iranian ballistic missile with cluster munition warhead struck Rishon Lezion, central Israel. Bomblets hit an empty kindergarten/daycare and multiple other sites across the city — 11 impact sites confirmed. Debris also fell near the Temple Mount/Al-Aqsa Mosque in Jerusalem's Old City. Minor casualties: man in 70s lightly injured, man in 40s treated. Education Minister Kisch cancelled all Passover holiday camps in 18 central Israel municipalities.
weaponType: Ballistic missile with cluster munition warhead

casualties.note: 2 lightly injured; empty daycare struck; debris near Al-Aqsa

description: Israeli airstrike targeted Malek Ashtar University of Technology's research and development complex in East Tehran. The IDF confirmed the target, stating the facility was used to develop components for nuclear weapons and ballistic missiles. Multiple explosions reported in eastern Tehran neighborhoods. The Malek Ashtar University is Iran's leading defense engineering institution and has been linked to IRGC missile, drone, and nuclear weaponization programs.

casualties.note: Casualties not confirmed — Iran has not released figures

description: Hezbollah rocket struck Misgav Am in northern Israel, killing civilian Ofer Moskovitz, 60. IDF opened investigation into whether Hezbollah projectile or friendly fire — no warning sirens sounded. Prompted Defense Minister Katz to order destruction of all Litani River bridges.

casualties.note: Ofer Moskovitz, 60, killed. IDF investigating origin.

description: Iran's 8th consecutive missile salvo of Day 23 targeted Arad and surrounding southern cities. Combined Day 23 toll: 150-180 injured across Arad, Dimona, Eilat, Beersheba, Kiryat Gat; 11 serious. Framed as retaliation for Natanz strike. Israeli air defenses successfully intercepted majority in later salvos.

casualties.note: 150-180 total Day 23 injuries; 11 serious. No deaths confirmed.

description: Iranian ballistic missiles targeted Beersheba as part of multi-salvo Day 23 campaign alongside Arad, Dimona, Eilat, Kiryat Gat. Israeli air defenses activated across the region. Structural damage limited compared to direct hits in Arad.

casualties.note: Part of coordinated 8-salvo campaign. Casualties included in combined count.

description: Iranian ballistic missile fitted with cluster warhead scattered submunitions across at least 7 sites in central Israel including the Tel Aviv metropolitan area. Cluster bomb fragments wounded 15 people across multiple neighborhoods. IDF confirmed cluster warhead fragments at multiple sites. A second cluster missile impact occurred March 23 with no new injuries. Iran's use of cluster munitions against civilian-populated urban areas marked an escalation in weapons types deployed.

strikeType: GBU-72 Advanced Penetrator (US Strike on Iran)
description: US forces dropped multiple GBU-72 Advanced Penetrator 5,000-pound bunker buster bombs on hardened underground Iranian anti-ship missile facilities along the coastline near the Strait of Hormuz. CENTCOM destroyed anti-ship cruise missile launchers, mobile missile systems, intelligence support sites, and ship-tracking radar relays. Part of Operation Epic Fury's campaign to degrade Iran's ability to threaten Hormuz shipping.
weaponType: GBU-72 Advanced Penetrator (5,000 lb bunker buster)

description: IDF overnight airstrikes destroyed 4 IRGC and Defense Ministry facilities dedicated to ballistic missile production in Tehran: an IRGC central missile component production compound, a missile parts storage facility, a Defense Ministry fuel production facility for ballistic missiles, and a second missile component production site. Power substations in western Tehran and Karaj were also struck, causing electrical outages across both cities.

Israeli airstrikes destroyed the Qasmiyeh Bridge, a major coastal artery linking Sidon with Tyre and southern Lebanon, as part of Defense Minister Katz's order to demolish all Litani River bridges used by Hezbollah for military logistics. Lebanese President Aoun warned the strikes constitute 'a prelude to a ground invasion.'

Israeli warplanes struck and heavily damaged the Zrarieh Bridge, a key Litani River crossing in southern Lebanon. This was the second bridge demolished in the same day as Israel executed Defense Minister Katz's order to destroy all Litani River crossings. The demolitions isolate southern Lebanon and signal potential expansion of IDF ground operations.

Hezbollah rocket struck the northern Israeli coastal city of Nahariya on Day 24, wounding six people. The attack is part of ongoing cross-border rocket and anti-tank fire as Israeli ground forces push deeper into southern Lebanon.

city: Tehran (IRGC HQ, Basij base, rocket factory)
description: IDF launched a 'wide-scale wave of strikes' on Tehran targeting IRGC police headquarters, Basij militia base, rocket engine production facility, and Isfahan Internal Security Force HQ. Al Jazeera's Tehran correspondent reported explosions 'unprecedented' in scale on the eastern side of the city.

CENTCOM struck and destroyed Iran's Qom Turbine Engine Production Plant, which manufactured gas turbine engines for IRGC attack drones and aircraft components. CENTCOM released before/after satellite imagery confirming the facility's total destruction.

US-Israeli airstrikes hit residential buildings in Tabriz, northwestern Iran, killing at least 6 people and injuring 6 others. East Azerbaijan Province Crisis Management Director Majid Farshi confirmed two separate deadly attacks on the city.
source: Al Jazeera — Unprecedented US-Israel strikes across Iran

description: Airstrike hit a residential area in Khorramabad, Lorestan province, killing at least one child whose body was pulled from rubble. Multiple others wounded. Confirmed by Fars News Agency.

US-Israeli strike hit a 100-kilowatt AM transmitter of the IRIB state radio station in Bandar Abbas, killing one IRIB security guard and injuring another. Bandar Abbas hosts Iran's primary naval base on the Strait of Hormuz.

CENTCOM confirmed US forces struck and destroyed the Qom Turbine Engine Production Plant, an IRGC facility manufacturing gas turbine engines for attack drones and aircraft components. CENTCOM released before/after satellite imagery confirming complete destruction. The facility had been photographed intact on March 6 and was destroyed by March 23.

Israeli airstrike hit the al-Shahabiya district of Tyre (Sur) in southern Lebanon, killing one civilian and wounding four others with moderate injuries. Confirmed by Lebanon's National News Agency (NNA). IDF simultaneously struck additional crossings on the Litani River, continuing its campaign to destroy all river crossing infrastructure.

IRGC launched ballistic missiles and drones targeting Prince Sultan Airbase in Saudi Arabia — primary US air hub in the region — and Naval Support Activity Bahrain (US 5th Fleet headquarters). Saudi Arabia confirmed two ballistic missiles were fired toward Riyadh: one intercepted by air defenses, a second fell in an uninhabited area south of the capital. The IRGC simultaneously threatened to mine all Persian Gulf access routes if Iran's coastline or islands were attacked.
source: Al Jazeera — Iran war Day 24 explainer

description: IDF conducted a targeted airstrike in Beirut that killed an identified IRGC Quds Force member — the first confirmed killing of an IRGC officer inside the Lebanese capital since the current conflict began. The IDF subsequently issued evacuation warnings for four southern Beirut suburbs: Bourj el-Barajneh, Hadath, Haret Hreik, and Chiyah, signaling expansion of Israeli targeting inside Beirut.

casualties.note: 1 IRGC Quds Force member killed

description: Iran fired cluster-warhead ballistic missiles toward northern Israel as part of a joint "integrated operation" with Hezbollah (~200 simultaneous rockets). Interception debris and a cluster munition warhead impacted a residential building in Nesher, a suburb south of Haifa. A man in his 30s was lightly injured after stepping on a fragment. IDF confirmed the suspicion of cluster munitions use. Air sirens blasted across northern Israel during the barrage.
source: Haaretz live blog — Iranian missile barrage causes damage in several central Israel locations

casualties.note: 1 man lightly injured by cluster munition fragment
